ap_physics_2_ray_diagrams_snell_s_law_: AP Physics 2: ray diagrams, Snell's law, mirrors, lenses, interference, diffraction, polarization. Side-by-side sims + calculators.
rainbows_mirages_after_images_color_po: Rainbows · mirages · after-images · color · polarized sky
50_optical_scientists_from_antiquity_t: 50+ optical scientists from antiquity to today
trivia_records_quotes_safety_data_teac: Trivia · records · quotes · safety · data · teaching tips
concept_progress_which_questions_you_h: Concept progress + which questions you have nailed
snell_s_law_sandbox_predict_tir_disper: Snell's law sandbox — predict TIR + dispersion
refraction_snell_s_law_total_internal_: 🌊 Refraction — Snell's law + total internal reflection
lenses_converging_diverging_thin_lens_: 🔍 Lenses — converging + diverging, thin lens equation
angle_exceeds_critical_angle_no_light_: Angle exceeds critical angle. No light transmitted — all reflects back. Basis of fiber optics, diamond brilliance, prism periscopes.
approaching_tir_boundary_refracted_ray: Approaching TIR boundary. Refracted ray runs nearly along the surface; transmitted intensity dropping.
light_entering_denser_medium_n_n_refra: Light entering denser medium (n₁ < n₂). Refracted ray bends TOWARD the normal — slower light in dense medium.
light_entering_less_dense_medium_refra: Light entering less-dense medium. Refracted ray bends AWAY from normal — faster light in light medium.
steep_angle_from_normal_much_energy_re: Steep angle from normal. Much energy reflected (Fresnel rises), little transmitted.
snell_s_law_inquiry_refraction_tir_dis: 🔬 Snell's Law Inquiry — Refraction, TIR, Dispersion
set_n_n_incidence_angle_and_wavelength: Set n₁, n₂, incidence angle, and wavelength. Predict where the system transitions from refraction to TIR. No score, no reveal.
your_hypothesis_where_exactly_does_tir: Your hypothesis (where exactly does TIR start, and why does diamond TIR more aggressively than glass?)
e_g_diamond_has_critical_angle_24_vs_g: e.g., diamond has critical angle ~24° vs glass ~42°, so any steeper angle TIRs...
why_is_there_no_critical_angle_when_go: Why is there NO critical angle when going from less-dense to more-dense?
set_water_air_n_1_33_n_1_00_what_s_the: Set water→air (n₁=1.33 n₂=1.00). What's the critical angle? Why do fish see the world compressed into a cone above?
in_a_rainbow_why_does_red_bend_less_th: In a rainbow, why does red bend less than blue? Connect to the wavelength slider.
how_does_a_prism_separate_white_light_: How does a prism separate white light if all wavelengths obey the same Snell's law?
i_can_explain_why_this_n_n_combination: I can explain why this n₁/n₂/θ combination yields this refraction state.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget — no score, no reveal, no answer dump. Dispersion model is illustrative (Cauchy-like first-order); real dispersion curves are material-specific (Sellmeier coefficients).
